rationale.rule: An anonymous block (*U…) whose entities are uniform small closed polylines confined to one material sub-layer named after a material token (here INSUL = insulation) under a '평면도' plan layer is a material hatch/fill fragment of the plan drawing — classify by the layer's material token, not as a reusable 심볼, a *D 치수캐시, or 가구.
rationale.counterfactual: If these same entities sat on a wall layer (e.g. *WALL / 벽 / W-WALL) or appeared as long 2-point segments spanning room edges, wall_likelihood would rise sharply and the role would stay 평면 부분도 as wall-bearing; SOLID arrowheads + MTEXT with a *D name would flip it to 치수캐시, and a furniture layer token (FURN/가구) would flip it to 가구.
rationale.uncertainty: Vertex coordinates are not provided (bbox not derivable — no LINE entities), so I cannot measure the polylines to confirm they are small hatch rectangles rather than thin wall-cavity outlines, nor verify whether this short insulation run (19 elements) traces exterior vs. party walls; the layer name alone carries the material semantics.
